Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and microalbumin/creatinine ratio, indicating significant renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting nephrotic-range proteinuria and possible tubular dysfunction. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is low, possibly due to anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Hyperchloremia is present. Findings are consistent with advanced kidney disease; urgent nephrology evaluation is recommended.
